name: ZeroTier (Software-Defined Networking)
pricing_notes: Free: 25 devices; Pro $5/mo for more; self-hostable controller

auth.notes: ZeroTier Central API token; local controller via zerotier-cli

performance.notes: P2P when possible; relay through ZeroTier servers otherwise

agent_readiness.notes: Software-defined networking creating virtual LANs over internet. Join network: zerotier-cli join <networkId>. Central API: POST /network, GET /network/{id}/member. Flow rules for traffic control. Bridge networks. No VPN server required. Peer-to-peer when possible. Up to 25 devices free. Self-host ZeroTier Central with ZeroNSD. Used for home lab access, dev environments, IoT device networking. Alternative to traditional VPN without gateway server.

gotchas: NAT traversal sometimes fails requiring relay (higher latency)
Flow rules (ZeroTier's traffic control language) complex to learn
25-device free limit reached quickly for larger deployments
best_when: Home lab remote access without dedicated VPN server
avoid_when: Large enterprise networks (Tailscale has better ACLs and SSO)
Compliance-requiring environments (Tailscale or WireGuard more auditable)
High-performance data transfer (traditional VPN may be faster)
summary: ZeroTier creates virtual LANs over the internet with P2P networking. zerotier-cli join <networkId>. No VPN server needed. Central API for network management. 25 devices free, self-hostable. Used for home lab access, dev environment networking, IoT. Tailscale for enterprise ACLs/SSO; ZeroTier for simpler P2P virtual networks.
